---
title: "EROP 2026 (June)"
authors: "Yuriel Ryan"
date: last-modified
---

# Introduction

Welcome to SYAI Labs. This is the official curriculum of the Early Research Opportunity Program (EROP) organized by the [Singapore Youth AI](https://sgyouthai.org/), supported by [AI Singapore](https://aisingapore.org/).

Across these pages, we provide examples for the learning objectives in this program (see the provided lesson plan). There will be positive and negative examples, as well as specific instances accompanying the ["I do, We do, You do"](mentoring-strategy.qmd) paradigm (to *distill knowledge* from the mentors). If there are any points/ideas that you are unsure about, do approach your assigned mentors during the program!

::: {.callout-tip}
Use the sidebar on the left to navigate. The program runs over five days; each day has its own page, and the worked examples build on one another from Day 1 through Day 5.
:::


---

# What's here

- [Research Brief --- Weapons of Mass Disruptions](research-brief.qmd) --- the open problem your team will work on.
- [Day 1 --- Kickoff and Problem Framing](day-1.qmd)
- [Day 2 --- Literature Review](day-2.qmd)
- [Day 3 --- Verify and Evaluate Information](day-3.qmd)
- [Day 4 --- Proposal Design](day-4.qmd)
- [Day 5 --- Proposal Write-ups and Presentation](day-5.qmd)
- [EROP Mentoring Strategy](mentoring-strategy.qmd) --- the "I Do, We Do, You Do" model for mentors.

---

# Future Iterations

![If you would like to join future iterations, kindly register your interest here!](/attachments/poster.png)
